text.title: Agent Rules and AI Gotchas for Attribution Models
text.summary: Concrete rules for agents building attribution pipelines, including variance thresholds, human-in-loop checkpoints, and failure modes specific to LLM agents.

section.title: Variance Thresholds

rule.statement: Always present three numbers per channel (platform-reported, GA4-modeled, warehouse-deduped) and flag if any two diverge by more than 15%.
rule.rationale: Below 15% variance is noise. Above 15% indicates a structural issue — mismatched windows, missing dedup key, or a tracking gap — that must be investigated before budget decisions.

section.reference: Python helper that computes platform-vs-warehouse and GA4-vs-warehouse divergence per channel and sets alert flag above 15%

rule.statement: Require human sign-off before any budget reallocation exceeding 10% of total spend, any change to the canonical attribution model, or any retroactive re-attribution.
rule.rationale: These actions have irreversible consequences: retroactive re-attribution destroys audit trail, model changes invalidate historical comparisons, and large reallocations cannot be easily undone.

list.item: Budget reallocation > 10% of total spend: human approval required
Change of canonical attribution model: human approval required
Retroactive re-attribution of historical conversions: snapshot first, human approval required
New geo-holdout test design: human approval required before budget changes execute

antipattern.description: Agent explains small variances (under 15%) with elaborate narratives before a minimum-variance threshold is met.
antipattern.reason: Small variances are noise from lookback window differences, timezone offsets, and sampling. Narratives on noise mislead decision-makers.

antipattern.description: Agent presents platform-reported and GA4-modeled numbers side-by-side as comparable values without labeling the model and window for each.
antipattern.reason: They use different lookback windows and deduplication logic. An unlabeled table implies comparability where none exists.

antipattern.description: Agent computes a single "today" number using GA4 property timezone and Google Ads account timezone interchangeably.
antipattern.reason: GA4 uses property timezone; Google Ads uses account timezone. These can differ by hours and produce off-by-one date errors in daily reports.

antipattern.description: Agent reports data-driven attribution point estimates without confidence intervals or sample-size caveats.
antipattern.reason: Data-driven models have uncertainty that grows with low conversion volume. Point estimates without CIs create false precision and overconfident recommendations.

rule.statement: When uploading offline conversions, SHA-256 hash lowercase-trimmed email addresses before sending; verify match rate is above 30% before declaring the upload successful.
rule.rationale: Agents that skip email normalization (lowercase + trim) see near-zero match rates silently. A match rate below 30% indicates a normalization or hashing bug.
